title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholder needs center on a lightweight, cordless handheld vacuum that reliably delivers at least 20 minutes of usable cleaning time in typical indoor EU home environments (10–40°C, up to 80% RH). Primary user goals are convenience (low mass <1.5 kg, easy handling/storage), adequate cleaning performance on common household debris and surfaces, and predictable runtime (including how it is measured and under what power mode/attachments). Key engineering constraints include battery safety and longevity, charging experience, acoustic comfort, maintainability (filter cleaning/replacement), and durability under humidity/temperature limits. Compliance needs include EU safety/regulatory conformity (CE) covering electrical safety and battery transport/charging protections; depending on features, this may also imply EMC, RoHS/REACH, WEEE, and relevant harmonized standards for household appliances and battery packs. Secondary stakeholders include retailers/service (returns, spares), manufacturing (cost/quality), and regulators (documentation, labeling, instructions).
clarification_questions: What minimum cleaning performance is required (e.g., target suction/airflow, debris types like crumbs/pet hair/fine dust, and surfaces such as upholstery, car seats, hard floors), and should performance be maintained as the battery discharges?
How should the 20+ minute runtime be defined and tested (power mode(s), with/without attachments, new vs aged battery, and acceptable end-of-run criterion such as loss of suction or auto-shutoff)?

What charging and battery expectations apply (charging method/connector and max charge time, removable vs built-in battery, desired battery chemistry, and any constraints on noise level or washable/replaceable filters)?

table.title: Battery-powered Handheld Vacuum Cleaner (EU Home Use) — System Requirements
rows: The vacuum cleaner SHALL provide at least two user-selectable power modes (Eco and Boost).
Eco and Boost modes are selectable via a dedicated control; mode selection changes motor input power by at least 30% relative difference.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL provide a usable runtime of at least 20 minutes in Eco mode on a fully charged battery.
Runtime measured from start at full charge to automatic shutdown at low-voltage cutoff is >= 20 min at 23 ± 2 °C with clean filter and standard nozzle.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL achieve an airflow of at least 12 L/s at the nozzle inlet in Boost mode.
Measured airflow at nozzle inlet is >= 12 L/s at 23 ± 2 °C with clean filter and standard nozzle.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL achieve a sealed suction of at least 8 kPa in Boost mode.
Measured maximum sealed suction at nozzle inlet is >= 8 kPa at 23 ± 2 °C with clean filter.
The fully assembled vacuum cleaner ready for use SHALL have a mass less than 1.5 kg.
Measured mass including installed battery and standard nozzle is < 1.50 kg.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL be operable by a user with one hand for continuous cleaning.
Trigger/button actuation force is <= 25 N and continuous operation is possible without requiring two-handed grip for >= 5 min.
The product SHALL charge from EU mains supply via an external charger or docking station.
Charging operates from 230 VAC ±10%, 50 Hz; full functionality demonstrated across this range.
The battery SHALL recharge from empty to 100% state-of-charge within 4 hours.
Charge time from low-voltage cutoff to charge complete indicator is <= 4.0 h at 23 ± 2 °C.
The battery pack and charger SHALL provide over-charge, over-discharge, over-current, and short-circuit protections.
Protections activate within manufacturer-specified thresholds and prevent venting, fire, or rupture during induced fault tests; no hazardous condition observed.
The product SHALL meet applicable EU safety requirements for household appliances and bear CE marking.
A complete EU Declaration of Conformity is available and a CE mark is present on product labeling; compliance evidence to applicable harmonized standards is documented.
The product SHALL comply with EU EMC requirements for residential environments.
Emissions and immunity tests pass to applicable EN standards with no loss of safety function; performance degradation limited to user-noticeable but recoverable behavior.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL operate within indoor ambient conditions typical for EU homes.
Meets performance requirements at 10 to 40 °C and 20% to 80% RH non-condensing without fault or safety shutdown.
The motorized system SHALL achieve a service life of at least 200 hours of run time without critical failure.
No critical failures occur over 200 h cumulative operation using a defined duty cycle (5 min on/5 min off) at 23 ± 2 °C.
The battery SHALL retain at least 80% of initial Eco-mode runtime after 300 full charge-discharge cycles.
After 300 cycles, measured Eco-mode runtime is >= 0.80 × initial runtime under the same test conditions.
The product SHALL include a removable, washable primary filter to support maintainability.
Filter can be removed and reinstalled without tools in <= 60 s and withstands 10 wash cycles with no tearing or loss of fit.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL limit accessible surface temperatures during operation to prevent burns.
Any user-accessible surface temperature is <= 48 °C after 15 min continuous operation at 23 ± 2 °C in Boost mode.
The product SHALL provide a low-battery indication before automatic shutdown.
Low-battery indicator activates at least 60 s before shutdown under steady Eco operation at 23 ± 2 °C.
The product materials and labeling SHALL support EU end-of-life and substance compliance obligations.
RoHS and REACH SVHC declarations are available; WEEE symbol is present and legible at >= 5 mm height on product or packaging.

versions.stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.

messages.content: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.

Stakeholders include: end users (homeowners/renters) prioritizing cleaning performance, portability (<1.5 kg), ergonomic comfort, low noise, easy emptying/maintenance; purchasers/gifters prioritizing price/value and reliability; EU regulators/market surveillance requiring compliance with applicable CE directives/regulations (e.g., Low Voltage Directive if applicable, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, WEEE, Battery Regulation, GPSR), safe battery charging/transport, and clear labeling/instructions; manufacturer/engineering focusing on meeting runtime >=20 min under defined test conditions, suction/airflow targets, battery selection (Li-ion), thermal management, durability, and manufacturability; service/support and retailers needing low return rates, replaceable consumables (filters), and spare parts; environmental stakeholders requiring recyclability and end-of-life handling (WEEE, battery take-back). Key requirement risks: runtime is test-condition dependent (power mode, floor type, battery age), weight definition (with/without battery/accessories), and EU safety scope (which standards apply: IEC/EN 60335 series, EN 62133/IEC 62133, UN 38.3, EMC EN 55014, etc.).
Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.

Stakeholder needs center on a lightweight, cordless handheld vacuum that reliably delivers at least 20 minutes of usable cleaning time in typical indoor EU home environments (10–40°C, up to 80% RH). Primary user goals are convenience (low mass <1.5 kg, easy handling/storage), adequate cleaning performance on common household debris and surfaces, and predictable runtime (including how it is measured and under what power mode/attachments). Key engineering constraints include battery safety and longevity, charging experience, acoustic comfort, maintainability (filter cleaning/replacement), and durability under humidity/temperature limits. Compliance needs include EU safety/regulatory conformity (CE) covering electrical safety and battery transport/charging protections; depending on features, this may also imply EMC, RoHS/REACH, WEEE, and relevant harmonized standards for household appliances and battery packs. Secondary stakeholders include retailers/service (returns, spares), manufacturing (cost/quality), and regulators (documentation, labeling, instructions).
